operator.note: MERGED-AWAY DUPLICATE of dof4/po (Llama-Guard misinformation channel): both reduce to 'the shared channel has no quality/truth-selectivity, so it propagates exploits as efficiently as discoveries.' Retained here only to mark the merge; the po item is the kept representative.

question: Borrow the epidemiology of contagion: model each improving idea as a pathogen spreading on the agent contact network, where a 'read/fetch event' is an exposure and an adopted technique is an infection. Fit an SIR-style transmission model to the real read/fetch logs and estimate an empirical R0 per technique. Recast 'collaboration drives progress' as the testable claim R0>1 for productive techniques. Then test the sharper claim: is transmission quality-SELECTIVE (R0_good > R0_bad) or quality-INDIFFERENT (R0 uncorrelated with technique quality) — i.e., is the platform an equally efficient vector for verifier-exploits/dead-end heuristics as for real discoveries, making 'collective intelligence' merely high-bandwidth copying with no quality selection?

floor.minimal_experiment: Data: the platform's read/fetch logs over a run, giving (agent, technique, timestamp) exposure events plus the adoption event when an agent first reuses a technique. Build the contact/exposure graph; for each technique fit a discrete-time SIR/Bass hazard (per-exposure adoption probability beta, recovery/abandonment gamma) and derive empirical R0 = beta/gamma. SETUP: independently label each technique's quality WITHOUT using its spread (the load-bearing requirement) — e.g. downstream verifier-score delta it produces, or held-out human/strong-judge rating of whether it is a real improvement vs a verifier-exploit/dead-end. QUANTITY MEASURED: the correlation/regression slope of R0 on quality label across techniques, and the gap R0_good - R0_bad. CONTROL: (i) a degree/exposure-frequency null — permute quality labels across techniques holding the contact graph and exposure counts fixed, to confirm any R0-quality slope exceeds what raw popularity/visibility produces; (ii) compare against techniques seeded equally often (matched initial exposure) so R0 differences are not just seeding artifacts.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: If the network is quality-selective, the regression slope of R0 on the independent quality label is significantly positive and R0_good - R0_bad > 0 beyond the label-permutation null. The reframe is FALSIFIED if R0 is statistically indistinguishable between high- and low-quality techniques (slope ~ 0, gap inside the permutation null) — meaning verifier-exploits spread as fast as real discoveries. Either outcome is informative and decision-changing.
floor.control: Label-permutation null (shuffle quality labels over the fixed contact graph + exposure counts) isolates quality-selectivity from raw popularity; an exposure-matched subset controls for seeding/visibility differences so R0 gaps are not artifacts of who-got-read-first.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Designers of multi-agent collaboration platforms and the frameshift 收敛/鉴价 station itself: a quality-indifferent R0 is direct evidence that propagation needs a quality-gating layer, not just more bandwidth. Also of interest to collective-intelligence / science-of-science researchers studying whether agent swarms select for truth.

prior_art.known_name: Epidemiological/SIR & Bass diffusion of ideas (Goffman-Newill, Daley-Kendall rumor model, Bass 1969); the quality-indifference twist is information cascades / herding (Bikhchandani-Hirshleifer-Welch) and relates to Goodhart when the 'pathogen' is a verifier-exploit
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known epidemiological-diffusion machinery operationalized in a new setting; the genuinely fresh move is not the SIR fit (textbook) but contrasting R0 against an EXTERNALLY-labelled quality axis to test whether an agent idea-network has any quality selectivity at all — turning 'collective intelligence' into a falsifiable claim rather than asserting it.
